author: davidleejy
text: pip install deepspeed fails if number of GPUs greater than a certain small number?

author: davidleejy
text:
### 🐛 Describe the bug

These attempts at installing fail 🥲:

Attempt 1:

```
pip install deepspeed --no-cache-dir
```

Attempt 2:

```
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7'
pip install deepspeed --no-cache-dir
```

These attempts succeed 🎉:

Attempt 3:

```
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='0,1,2,3'
pip install deepspeed --no-cache-dir
```

Attempt 4:

```
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0
pip install deepspeed --no-cache-dir
```

author: malfet
text:
I can not reproduce it on a very similar AWS system.  
Can you please share the output of `nvidia-smi` ?  
And please try running something like: `python -c "import torch;print(torch._C._cuda_getDeviceCount())"`  
(I suspect nvidia-smi would show HW errors with some of the GPUs you have on the system)

author: davidleejy
text: Wait.. this could indicate an issue on the machine I'm on. I feel like the device count should show 8 instead of 6 since the machine I'm using has eight GPUS installed..🥹

author: skullcrossbones
text: Could you please provide the vbios? Thank you.

author: wbigat

wbigatadded a commit that references this issue on Mar 28, 2024Don't cache device_count if we haven't initialized CUDA yet (pytorch#…...0284bca
Don't cache device_count if we haven't initialized CUDA yet (#122815)

Before initializing CUDA, it can change by modifying CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES

Fixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/122085
Fixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/38616
Fixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/110000
Fixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/110971
Fixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/95073

Signed-off-by: Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>
Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/122815
Approved by: https://github.com/albanD
